# Nightly — scheduled build of `main` producing a dated, signed
# pre-release.
#
# Complements `latest.yml`:
#   - `latest` (rolling): rebuilt on every push to main; useful for
#     always-fresh bleeding-edge binaries. Tag is force-replaced.
#   - `nightly-YYYY-MM-DD` (dated): rebuilt once per day at a fixed time;
#     useful when you want a stable reference point to pin to, reproduce
#     bugs against, or bisect regressions over several days.
#
# Retention: only the 7 most recent nightly-* releases are kept so the
# Releases page stays readable.

name: nightly

on:
  schedule:
    # 07:00 UTC = midnight PT, 2am CT, 3am ET. Pick a time when main is
    # least likely to be mid-push.
    - cron: "0 7 * * *"
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: nightly
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always

jobs:
  prepare:
    name: prepare nightly metadata
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tag }}
      should_run: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.should_run }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 2
      - name: compute tag + skip-if-no-changes
        id: meta
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          TAG="nightly-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"
          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # On the scheduled run, skip if main hasn't moved in the last 24h
          # (otherwise we rebuild identical bits every day). Manual dispatch
          # always runs.
          if [[ "$EVENT_NAME" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
            echo "should_run=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          LAST_COMMIT_EPOCH=$(git log -1 --format=%ct origin/main)
          NOW=$(date -u +%s)
          if (( NOW - LAST_COMMIT_EPOCH > 86400 )); then
            echo "no commits in last 24h; skipping nightly"
            echo "should_run=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "should_run=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  build-cli:
    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
    needs: prepare
    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - { target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,  os: ubuntu-latest,    label: linux-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
          - { target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, os: ubuntu-24.04-arm, label: linux-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
          # macos-latest + cross-compile: macos-13 Intel runners unavailable.
          - { target: x86_64-apple-darwin,       os: macos-latest,     label: macos-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
          - { target: aarch64-apple-darwin,      os: macos-latest,     label: macos-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
          - { target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,    os: windows-latest,   label: windows-x86_64, archive: zip }
    env:
      TAG: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}
      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
      LABEL: ${{ matrix.label }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - shell: bash
        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: nightly-cli-${{ matrix.target }}

      - shell: bash
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server

      - name: stage artifact (unix)
        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          NAME="orpheus-$TAG-$LABEL"
          mkdir -p "staging/$NAME"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$NAME/"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$NAME/"
          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$NAME.tar.gz" "$NAME" )
          echo "ARTIFACT=$NAME" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: stage artifact (windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $NAME = "orpheus-$env:TAG-$env:LABEL"
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$NAME"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$NAME/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$NAME.zip"
          "ARTIFACT=$NAME" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ env.ARTIFACT }}
          path: staging/${{ env.ARTIFACT }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  build-tauri:
    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
    needs: prepare
    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - { name: macos-universal, os: macos-latest,   args: "--target universal-apple-darwin", rust-targets: "aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin" }
          - { name: linux-x86_64,    os: ubuntu-22.04,   args: "",                                rust-targets: "" }
          - { name: windows-x86_64,  os: windows-latest, args: "",                                rust-targets: "" }
    env:
      RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust targets
        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
        shell: bash
        run: |
          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
          shared-key: nightly-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}

      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps

      - run: mise run web:install
      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
          args: ${{ matrix.args }}

      - name: collect tauri bundles
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tauri-output
          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
          # Layout varies by target triple:
          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
          # ones.
          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;
          ls -la tauri-output/
          if [ -z "$(ls -A tauri-output/)" ]; then
            echo "::error::no Tauri bundles found under target/**/release/bundle"
            find target -type d -name bundle 2>&1 | head -20 || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
          path: tauri-output/*
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish:
    name: publish nightly pre-release
    needs: [prepare, build-cli, build-tauri]
    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      TAG: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
      RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: list artifacts
        run: ls -la dist/

      - name: sign artifacts with gpg
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          artifacts-dir: dist
          gpg-private-key: ${{ secrets.GPG_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          gpg-passphrase: ${{ secrets.GPG_PASSPHRASE }}
          gpg-key-id: ${{ secrets.GPG_KEY_ID }}

      - name: delete prior nightly release with same date (re-run safety)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: gh release delete "$TAG" --yes --cleanup-tag || true

      - name: create nightly pre-release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.TAG }}
          name: "Nightly ${{ env.TAG }}"
          prerelease: true
          make_latest: "false"
          body: |
            **Scheduled nightly build from `main`.** Dated and immutable —
            a new nightly is cut once per day. For a stable version, use a
            tagged release.

            - date:   `${{ env.TAG }}`
            - commit: `${{ github.sha }}`
            - run:    `${{ github.run_id }}`

            All artifacts are GPG-signed. Verify with:
            ```
            gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
            sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
            ```
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

      - name: prune old nightly releases (keep last 7)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # List nightly-* releases sorted newest-first, skip the 7 most recent,
          # delete the rest.
          gh release list --limit 200 --json tagName,createdAt \
            --jq '[.[] | select(.tagName | startswith("nightly-"))] | sort_by(.createdAt) | reverse | .[7:] | .[].tagName' \
            | while read -r OLD; do
                [ -z "$OLD" ] && continue
                echo "deleting old nightly: $OLD"
                gh release delete "$OLD" --yes --cleanup-tag || true
              done

  url "https://github.com/%%REPO%%/releases/download/v#{version}/Orpheus_#{version}_#{arch == :arm64 ? "aarch64" : "x64"}.dmg",
      verified: "github.com/%%REPO%%/"
  name "Orpheus"
  desc "Recover lost cryptocurrency from forgotten wallets"
  homepage "https://github.com/%%REPO%%"

  livecheck do
    url :url
    strategy :github_latest
  end

  auto_updates false
  depends_on macos: ">= :monterey"

  app "Orpheus.app"

  caveats <<~EOS
    Orpheus is not yet signed with an Apple Developer ID. On first launch
    macOS will block it. To run:

      xattr -cr /Applications/Orpheus.app

    Then open from Applications normally. This caveat will be removed
    once Apple code signing is in place.
  EOS

  zap trash: [
    "~/Library/Application Support/com.orpheus.desktop",
    "~/Library/Preferences/com.orpheus.desktop.plist",
    "~/Library/Logs/Orpheus",
  ]
end

1	# Releasing Orpheus
2	
3	This doc is the runbook for cutting a release, the signing setup behind
4	it, and the placeholders that turn on platform-specific signing when
5	their accounts become available.
6	
7	## Release channels
8	
9	| Channel                 | Workflow              | Trigger                       | Tag shape            | GitHub "latest"? |
10	|-------------------------|-----------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------|------------------|
11	| Stable                  | `release.yml`         | push `v*` tag / manual        | `v0.1.0`             | ✅ yes           |
12	| Rolling pre-release     | `latest.yml`          | push to `main`                | `latest`             | ❌ pre-release   |
13	| Nightly (dated)         | `nightly.yml`         | cron `0 7 * * *` / manual     | `nightly-YYYY-MM-DD` | ❌ pre-release   |
14	
15	`latest` rebuilds on every push, `nightly-*` rebuilds once a day. Both
16	are pre-releases so GitHub's "Latest release" pointer only ever moves
17	when a stable `v*` tag is cut.
18	
19	## Cutting a stable release
20	
21	```bash
22	# 1. Bump versions + run CI locally.
23	#    The single source of truth is `version = "X.Y.Z"` in Cargo.toml
24	#    under [workspace.package]. All crates inherit it via
25	#    `version.workspace = true`, so one edit covers every crate.
26	#    Use cargo-release for the tag+commit in one go:
27	cargo install cargo-release   # one-time
28	cargo release 0.1.1 --workspace --execute --no-publish --no-push
29	
30	# 2. Push the commit + tag. The tag push triggers release.yml.
31	git push origin main --follow-tags
32	```
33	
34	The `release.yml` workflow will:
35	
36	1. Create a draft GitHub release.
37	2. Build CLI + server binaries for 5 targets; build Tauri bundles for
38	   macOS (universal), Linux x86_64, Windows x86_64.
39	3. GPG-sign every artifact and produce a detached-signed `SHA256SUMS`.
40	4. Attach artifacts to the draft and flip it to published + latest.
41	5. Publish `orpheus-core`, `orpheus-cli`, `orpheus-server` to crates.io.
42	6. Update the Homebrew cask in `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus`.
43	
44	Failure anywhere in steps 1–4 leaves the draft untouched — re-run the
45	workflow. Failure in 5 or 6 does not block the GitHub release; rerun the
46	specific sub-workflow (`publish-crates` / `publish-cask`) manually via
47	`gh workflow run`.
48	
49	## Linux self-signing (GPG)
50	
51	All Linux artifacts — plus the tarballs, zips, DMGs, and MSIs — are
52	signed with a long-lived GPG signing key. Users verify with:
53	
54	```bash
55	curl -L https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/raw/main/packaging/gpg/orpheus-release-pubkey.asc \
56	  | gpg --import
57	
58	# Per-artifact
59	gpg --verify orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb.asc orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb
60	
61	# Or one-shot via the signed manifest
62	gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
63	sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
64	```
65	
66	### One-time key setup
67	
68	```bash
69	# 1. Generate a 4096-bit RSA master + signing subkey. On the interactive
70	#    prompts pick: RSA+RSA, 4096 bits, expiry 2y.
71	gpg --full-generate-key
72	#    UID: "Orpheus Release Signing <releases@orpheus.dev>"
73	
74	# 2. Identify the key IDs.
75	gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=LONG
76	
77	# 3. Export the public key into the repo.
78	gpg --armor --export <MASTER_KEY_ID> \
79	  > packaging/gpg/orpheus-release-pubkey.asc
80	
81	# 4. Export ONLY the signing subkey (never the master) for CI. The `!`
82	#    after the subkey ID tells gpg to export that subkey only.
83	gpg --armor --export-secret-subkeys <SUBKEY_ID>! | base64 > ci-key.b64
84	
85	# 5. Load into GitHub secrets:
86	gh secret set GPG_PRIVATE_KEY < ci-key.b64
87	gh secret set GPG_PASSPHRASE --body "<subkey passphrase>"
88	gh secret set GPG_KEY_ID     --body "<SUBKEY_ID>"
89	rm ci-key.b64
90	
91	# 6. Publish the public key to a keyserver so casual users can find it.
92	gpg --keyserver keys.openpgp.org --send-keys <MASTER_KEY_ID>
93	```
94	
95	### Key storage best practices
96	
97	- Keep the **master key offline** (paper backup + air-gapped USB stick).
98	  Rotate the subkey yearly; the master stays stable for 2+ years.
99	- CI only ever holds the signing subkey. Losing it means revoking the
100	  subkey and issuing a new one — not rotating the whole identity.
101	- For the highest-value stable releases, consider moving the master key
102	  onto a YubiKey and signing manually from a local machine before
103	  uploading. The rolling `latest`/`nightly` CI signatures stay automated.
104	
105	## crates.io publishing
106	
107	The tagged `release.yml` calls `publish-crates.yml`. Bootstrap once:
108	
109	```bash
110	# 1. Generate a token scoped to publishing the three crates (NOT account-wide).
111	#    Visit https://crates.io/settings/tokens -> "New token".
112	#    Scopes: publish-new, publish-update
113	#    Crate filter: orpheus-core, orpheus-cli, orpheus-server
114	
115	gh secret set CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN --body "cio_..."
116	
117	# 2. Enable 2FA on your crates.io account.
118	```
119	
120	Publishing order (handled automatically by the workflow):
121	
122	1. `orpheus-core`
123	2. `orpheus-cli`   (depends on core; `--no-verify` to skip redundant rebuild)
124	3. `orpheus-server` (same)
125	
126	`orpheus-tauri` and `orpheus-demo-fixtures` are marked `publish = false`
127	and skipped.
128	
129	Users can install without compiling via
130	[`cargo-binstall`](https://github.com/cargo-bins/cargo-binstall), which
131	downloads the matching archive from GitHub Releases:
132	
133	```bash
134	cargo binstall orpheus-cli
135	```
136	
137	The `[package.metadata.binstall]` block in each crate's `Cargo.toml`
138	points at the release artifact naming scheme.
139	
140	## Homebrew cask
141	
142	The first release must bootstrap the tap by hand; afterwards
143	`publish-cask.yml` keeps it current automatically.
144	
145	```bash
146	# 1. Create the tap repo.
147	gh repo create dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus --public --add-readme \
148	  --description "Homebrew tap for Orpheus"
149	cd /tmp && git clone git@github.com:dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus.git
150	cd homebrew-orpheus && mkdir Casks
151	printf 'cask "orpheus" do\n  version "0.0.0"\nend\n' > Casks/orpheus.rb
152	git add . && git commit -m "init tap" && git push
153	
154	# 2. Create a PAT scoped to the tap repo and store as HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN.
155	#    Fine-grained token, repo: dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus, permissions:
156	#    Contents: Read and write.
157	gh secret set HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN --body "<pat>"
158	```
159	
160	Users then install with:
161	
162	```bash
163	brew tap dougnukem/orpheus
164	brew install --cask orpheus
165	```
166	
167	The cask template ships a `caveats` block explaining the one-time
168	`xattr -cr /Applications/Orpheus.app` required on unsigned builds.
169	Delete that block from the two templates in
170	[packaging/homebrew/](../packaging/homebrew/) once Apple signing goes
171	live.
172	
173	## Windows signing — SignPath Foundation (placeholder)
174	
175	The release workflow contains a commented-out SignPath block ready to
176	enable once the Foundation application is approved.
177	
178	### Application checklist
179	
180	- MIT/Apache-2.0 license (✅ already MIT)
181	- Public repo with non-trivial history (✅)
182	- Tagged release + changelog (✅ once the first v* tag is cut)
183	- Reproducible build via GitHub Actions (✅)
184	- Homepage describing the app (✅ the README)
185	
186	Apply at [signpath.io/foundation](https://about.signpath.io/foundation).
187	Review takes ~1–2 weeks.
188	
189	### After approval
190	
191	1. On signpath.io: create project `orpheus`, signing policy
192	   `release-signing`, and link it to this repo.
193	2. Create an API token (`Settings → API Tokens → Add`) and store it:
194	
195	   ```bash
196	   gh secret set  SIGNPATH_API_TOKEN --body "<token>"
197	   gh variable set SIGNPATH_ORG_ID   --body "<org-uuid>"
198	   ```
199	
200	3. Uncomment the `signpath/github-action-submit-signing-request@v1` steps
201	   in `release.yml` (both `build-cli` and `build-tauri` jobs).
202	4. Remove any Windows-signing disclaimers from the README.
203	
204	SignPath signs unsigned artifacts uploaded to GitHub Actions, then
205	returns signed versions. Our build steps already produce unsigned
206	artifacts, so only the signing call needs to be enabled.
207	
208	## macOS signing — Apple Developer ID (placeholder)
209	
210	Tauri's bundler already reads a full set of `APPLE_*` env vars. The
211	`build-tauri` job in `release.yml` passes them through; they are simply
212	unset today, so bundling proceeds unsigned. CLI binaries have a matching
213	commented-out block in `build-cli`.
214	
215	### Once enrolled ($99/yr)
216	
217	1. Create a **Developer ID Application** certificate at
218	   [developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates).
219	2. Export the cert + private key as a `.p12` with a password.
220	3. Create an App Store Connect API key at
221	   [appstoreconnect.apple.com/access/integrations/api](https://appstoreconnect.apple.com/access/integrations/api).
222	   Download the `.p8`, note the Key ID and Issuer ID.
223	4. Populate secrets:
224	
225	   ```bash
226	   base64 -i cert.p12 | gh secret set APPLE_CERTIFICATE
227	   gh secret set APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD --body "<p12 password>"
228	   gh secret set APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY     --body "Developer ID Application: <Your Name> (<TEAM>)"
229	   gh secret set APPLE_TEAM_ID              --body "<TEAM>"
230	   base64 -i AuthKey_XXX.p8 | gh secret set APPLE_API_KEY
231	   gh secret set APPLE_API_KEY_ID           --body "<KEY_ID>"
232	   gh secret set APPLE_API_ISSUER           --body "<ISSUER_UUID>"
233	   ```
234	
235	5. Uncomment the CLI signing/notarization blocks in `release.yml`
236	   (`build-cli`), and remove the Gatekeeper `caveats` block from both
237	   Homebrew cask templates in `packaging/homebrew/`.
238	6. Rerun `release.yml` via `workflow_dispatch` on the existing tag to
239	   replace the unsigned artifacts with signed ones.
240	
241	## Tauri updater signing (minisign)
242	
243	Separate from OS signing. Used only if we enable the Tauri auto-updater.
244	
245	```bash
246	cargo install tauri-cli --version '^2'
247	cargo tauri signer generate -w ~/.tauri/orpheus-updater.key
248	# Store the private key + passphrase:
249	base64 -i ~/.tauri/orpheus-updater.key \
250	  | gh secret set TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY
251	gh secret set TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD --body "<passphrase>"
252	# The matching public key should be embedded in tauri.conf.json -> bundle.pubkey
253	# before enabling the updater. See https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/updater/
254	```
255	
256	The env vars are already plumbed through `release.yml`; the updater
257	itself is off until `tauri.conf.json` references the public key.
258	
259	## Secret + variable cheat-sheet
260	
261	| Name                                 | Kind     | Required for         | Notes                                   |
262	|--------------------------------------|----------|----------------------|-----------------------------------------|
263	| `GPG_PRIVATE_KEY`                    | secret   | all sign steps       | base64-encoded ASCII armor of subkey    |
264	| `GPG_PASSPHRASE`                     | secret   | all sign steps       | passphrase for the subkey               |
265	| `GPG_KEY_ID`                         | secret   | all sign steps       | long key ID or fingerprint of subkey    |
266	| `CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN`               | secret   | publish-crates.yml   | scoped crates.io token                  |
267	| `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN`                 | secret   | publish-cask.yml     | PAT with write on the tap repo          |
268	| `SIGNPATH_API_TOKEN`                 | secret   | Windows signing      | add when SignPath is live               |
269	| `SIGNPATH_ORG_ID`                    | variable | Windows signing      | org UUID from signpath.io               |
270	| `APPLE_CERTIFICATE`                  | secret   | Apple signing        | base64 .p12                             |
271	| `APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD`         | secret   | Apple signing        |                                         |
272	| `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`             | secret   | Apple signing        | e.g. `Developer ID Application: ...`    |
273	| `APPLE_TEAM_ID`                      | secret   | Apple signing        |                                         |
274	| `APPLE_ID` / `APPLE_PASSWORD`        | secret   | Apple legacy notary  | prefer API key below                    |
275	| `APPLE_API_KEY`                      | secret   | Apple notarization   | base64 .p8                              |
276	| `APPLE_API_KEY_ID`                   | secret   | Apple notarization   |                                         |
277	| `APPLE_API_ISSUER`                   | secret   | Apple notarization   |                                         |
278	| `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY`          | secret   | Tauri updater        |                                         |
279	| `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD` | secret   | Tauri updater        |                                         |
280	
281	## Running workflows locally with `act`
282	
283	The publishing workflows are `ubuntu-latest` jobs, so
284	[nektos/act](https://github.com/nektos/act) can run them in a local
285	Docker container — useful for smoke-testing secret wiring and template
286	rendering before pushing a real tag.
287	
288	```bash
289	brew install act
290	cp .secrets.example .secrets      # gitignored; fill in real values
291	# Cask dry-run against a ref that exists (main) but no release artifacts.
292	# Validates HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN checkout works; render step fails as expected.
293	act workflow_dispatch \
294	  -W .github/workflows/publish-cask.yml \
295	  --input tag=main
296	```
297	
298	Defaults for `act` (runner image + `--secret-file .secrets`) are wired up
299	in the repo's [.actrc](../.actrc). Personal overrides go in
300	`.actrc.local` (gitignored).
301	
302	If you use 1Password, skip the plaintext `.secrets` file entirely:
303	
304	```bash
305	op inject -i .secrets.op | act workflow_dispatch \
306	  -W .github/workflows/publish-cask.yml \
307	  --input tag=main \
308	  --secret-file /dev/stdin
309	```
310	
311	## Troubleshooting
312	
313	- **Tag push didn't trigger `release.yml`** — check the tag matches
314	  `v*`, and that the workflow is enabled. Re-run via
315	  `gh workflow run release.yml -f tag=v0.1.1` as a fallback.
316	- **GPG step fails with "no secret key"** — the `GPG_PRIVATE_KEY`
317	  secret must be the base64 of a private key that matches `GPG_KEY_ID`.
318	  Regenerate from the source key with the exact `<SUBKEY_ID>!` syntax.
319	- **cargo publish: "crate already uploaded"** — idempotent; the workflow
320	  detects this and skips. A real version bump fixes it.
321	- **Homebrew cask fails to download** — the `sha256` mismatched. Either
322	  the release artifact naming changed (check `tauri.conf.json` product
323	  name) or the artifact wasn't uploaded. Re-run `publish-cask.yml`.
324	- **Nightly skipped with "no commits in last 24h"** — by design. Use
325	  `workflow_dispatch` to force a build.
326

# Releasing Orpheus

This doc is the runbook for cutting a release, the signing setup behind
it, and the placeholders that turn on platform-specific signing when
their accounts become available.

## Release channels

| Channel                 | Workflow              | Trigger                       | Tag shape            | GitHub "latest"? |
|-------------------------|-----------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------|------------------|
| Stable                  | `release.yml`         | push `v*` tag / manual        | `v0.1.0`             | ✅ yes           |
| Rolling pre-release     | `latest.yml`          | push to `main`                | `latest`             | ❌ pre-release   |
| Nightly (dated)         | `nightly.yml`         | cron `0 7 * * *` / manual     | `nightly-YYYY-MM-DD` | ❌ pre-release   |

`latest` rebuilds on every push, `nightly-*` rebuilds once a day. Both
are pre-releases so GitHub's "Latest release" pointer only ever moves
when a stable `v*` tag is cut.

## Cutting a stable release

```bash
# 1. Bump versions + run CI locally.
#    The single source of truth is `version = "X.Y.Z"` in Cargo.toml
#    under [workspace.package]. All crates inherit it via
#    `version.workspace = true`, so one edit covers every crate.
#    Use cargo-release for the tag+commit in one go:
cargo install cargo-release   # one-time
cargo release 0.1.1 --workspace --execute --no-publish --no-push

# 2. Push the commit + tag. The tag push triggers release.yml.
git push origin main --follow-tags
```

The `release.yml` workflow will:

1. Create a draft GitHub release.
2. Build CLI + server binaries for 5 targets; build Tauri bundles for
   macOS (universal), Linux x86_64, Windows x86_64.
3. GPG-sign every artifact and produce a detached-signed `SHA256SUMS`.
4. Attach artifacts to the draft and flip it to published + latest.
5. Publish `orpheus-core`, `orpheus-cli`, `orpheus-server` to crates.io.
6. Update the Homebrew cask in `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus`.

Failure anywhere in steps 1–4 leaves the draft untouched — re-run the
workflow. Failure in 5 or 6 does not block the GitHub release; rerun the
specific sub-workflow (`publish-crates` / `publish-cask`) manually via
`gh workflow run`.

## Linux self-signing (GPG)

All Linux artifacts — plus the tarballs, zips, DMGs, and MSIs — are
signed with a long-lived GPG signing key. Users verify with:

```bash
curl -L https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/raw/main/packaging/gpg/orpheus-release-pubkey.asc \
  | gpg --import

# Per-artifact
gpg --verify orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb.asc orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb

# Or one-shot via the signed manifest
gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
```

### One-time key setup

```bash
# 1. Generate a 4096-bit RSA master + signing subkey. On the interactive
#    prompts pick: RSA+RSA, 4096 bits, expiry 2y.
gpg --full-generate-key
#    UID: "Orpheus Release Signing <releases@orpheus.dev>"

# 2. Identify the key IDs.
gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=LONG

# 3. Export the public key into the repo.
gpg --armor --export <MASTER_KEY_ID> \
  > packaging/gpg/orpheus-release-pubkey.asc

# 4. Export ONLY the signing subkey (never the master) for CI. The `!`
#    after the subkey ID tells gpg to export that subkey only.
gpg --armor --export-secret-subkeys <SUBKEY_ID>! | base64 > ci-key.b64

# 5. Load into GitHub secrets:
gh secret set GPG_PRIVATE_KEY < ci-key.b64
gh secret set GPG_PASSPHRASE --body "<subkey passphrase>"
gh secret set GPG_KEY_ID     --body "<SUBKEY_ID>"
rm ci-key.b64

# 6. Publish the public key to a keyserver so casual users can find it.
gpg --keyserver keys.openpgp.org --send-keys <MASTER_KEY_ID>
```

### Key storage best practices

- Keep the **master key offline** (paper backup + air-gapped USB stick).
  Rotate the subkey yearly; the master stays stable for 2+ years.
- CI only ever holds the signing subkey. Losing it means revoking the
  subkey and issuing a new one — not rotating the whole identity.
- For the highest-value stable releases, consider moving the master key
  onto a YubiKey and signing manually from a local machine before
  uploading. The rolling `latest`/`nightly` CI signatures stay automated.

## crates.io publishing

The tagged `release.yml` calls `publish-crates.yml`. Bootstrap once:

```bash
# 1. Generate a token scoped to publishing the three crates (NOT account-wide).
#    Visit https://crates.io/settings/tokens -> "New token".
#    Scopes: publish-new, publish-update
#    Crate filter: orpheus-core, orpheus-cli, orpheus-server

gh secret set CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN --body "cio_..."

# 2. Enable 2FA on your crates.io account.
```

Publishing order (handled automatically by the workflow):

1. `orpheus-core`
2. `orpheus-cli`   (depends on core; `--no-verify` to skip redundant rebuild)
3. `orpheus-server` (same)

`orpheus-tauri` and `orpheus-demo-fixtures` are marked `publish = false`
and skipped.

Users can install without compiling via
[`cargo-binstall`](https://github.com/cargo-bins/cargo-binstall), which
downloads the matching archive from GitHub Releases:

```bash
cargo binstall orpheus-cli
```

The `[package.metadata.binstall]` block in each crate's `Cargo.toml`
points at the release artifact naming scheme.

```bash

gh repo create dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus --public --add-readme \
  --description "Homebrew tap for Orpheus"
cd /tmp && git clone git@github.com:dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus.git
cd homebrew-orpheus && mkdir Casks
printf 'cask "orpheus" do\n  version "0.0.0"\nend\n' > Casks/orpheus.rb
git add . && git commit -m "init tap" && git push

# 2. Create a PAT scoped to the tap repo and store as HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN.
#    Fine-grained token, repo: dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus, permissions:
#    Contents: Read and write.
gh secret set HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN --body "<pat>"
```

Users then install with:

```bash
brew tap dougnukem/orpheus
brew install --cask orpheus
```

`xattr -cr /Applications/Orpheus.app` required on unsigned builds.

### Once enrolled ($99/yr)

1. Create a **Developer ID Application** certificate at
   [developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates).
2. Export the cert + private key as a `.p12` with a password.
3. Create an App Store Connect API key at
   [appstoreconnect.apple.com/access/integrations/api](https://appstoreconnect.apple.com/access/integrations/api).
   Download the `.p8`, note the Key ID and Issuer ID.
4. Populate secrets:

   ```bash
   base64 -i cert.p12 | gh secret set APPLE_CERTIFICATE
   gh secret set APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD --body "<p12 password>"
   gh secret set APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY     --body "Developer ID Application: <Your Name> (<TEAM>)"
   gh secret set APPLE_TEAM_ID              --body "<TEAM>"
   base64 -i AuthKey_XXX.p8 | gh secret set APPLE_API_KEY
   gh secret set APPLE_API_KEY_ID           --body "<KEY_ID>"
   gh secret set APPLE_API_ISSUER           --body "<ISSUER_UUID>"
   ```

5. Uncomment the CLI signing/notarization blocks in `release.yml`
   (`build-cli`), and remove the Gatekeeper `caveats` block from both
   Homebrew cask templates in `packaging/homebrew/`.
6. Rerun `release.yml` via `workflow_dispatch` on the existing tag to
   replace the unsigned artifacts with signed ones.

## Tauri updater signing (minisign)

Separate from OS signing. Used only if we enable the Tauri auto-updater.

```bash
cargo install tauri-cli --version '^2'
cargo tauri signer generate -w ~/.tauri/orpheus-updater.key
# Store the private key + passphrase:
base64 -i ~/.tauri/orpheus-updater.key \
  | gh secret set TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY
gh secret set TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD --body "<passphrase>"
# The matching public key should be embedded in tauri.conf.json -> bundle.pubkey
# before enabling the updater. See https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/updater/
```

The env vars are already plumbed through `release.yml`; the updater
itself is off until `tauri.conf.json` references the public key.

## Secret + variable cheat-sheet

| Name                                 | Kind     | Required for         | Notes                                   |
|--------------------------------------|----------|----------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| `GPG_PRIVATE_KEY`                    | secret   | all sign steps       | base64-encoded ASCII armor of subkey    |
| `GPG_PASSPHRASE`                     | secret   | all sign steps       | passphrase for the subkey               |
| `GPG_KEY_ID`                         | secret   | all sign steps       | long key ID or fingerprint of subkey    |
| `CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN`               | secret   | publish-crates.yml   | scoped crates.io token                  |
| `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN`                 | secret   | publish-cask.yml     | PAT with write on the tap repo          |
| `SIGNPATH_API_TOKEN`                 | secret   | Windows signing      | add when SignPath is live               |
| `SIGNPATH_ORG_ID`                    | variable | Windows signing      | org UUID from signpath.io               |
| `APPLE_CERTIFICATE`                  | secret   | Apple signing        | base64 .p12                             |
| `APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD`         | secret   | Apple signing        |                                         |
| `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY`             | secret   | Apple signing        | e.g. `Developer ID Application: ...`    |
| `APPLE_TEAM_ID`                      | secret   | Apple signing        |                                         |
| `APPLE_ID` / `APPLE_PASSWORD`        | secret   | Apple legacy notary  | prefer API key below                    |
| `APPLE_API_KEY`                      | secret   | Apple notarization   | base64 .p8                              |
| `APPLE_API_KEY_ID`                   | secret   | Apple notarization   |                                         |
| `APPLE_API_ISSUER`                   | secret   | Apple notarization   |                                         |
| `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY`          | secret   | Tauri updater        |                                         |
| `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD` | secret   | Tauri updater        |                                         |

## Running workflows locally with `act`

The publishing workflows are `ubuntu-latest` jobs, so
[nektos/act](https://github.com/nektos/act) can run them in a local
Docker container — useful for smoke-testing secret wiring and template
rendering before pushing a real tag.

```bash
brew install act
cp .secrets.example .secrets      # gitignored; fill in real values
# Cask dry-run against a ref that exists (main) but no release artifacts.
# Validates HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN checkout works; render step fails as expected.
act workflow_dispatch \
  -W .github/workflows/publish-cask.yml \
  --input tag=main
```

Defaults for `act` (runner image + `--secret-file .secrets`) are wired up
in the repo's [.actrc](../.actrc). Personal overrides go in
`.actrc.local` (gitignored).

If you use 1Password, skip the plaintext `.secrets` file entirely:

```bash
op inject -i .secrets.op | act workflow_dispatch \
  -W .github/workflows/publish-cask.yml \
  --input tag=main \
  --secret-file /dev/stdin
```

## Troubleshooting

- **Tag push didn't trigger `release.yml`** — check the tag matches
  `v*`, and that the workflow is enabled. Re-run via
  `gh workflow run release.yml -f tag=v0.1.1` as a fallback.
- **GPG step fails with "no secret key"** — the `GPG_PRIVATE_KEY`
  secret must be the base64 of a private key that matches `GPG_KEY_ID`.
  Regenerate from the source key with the exact `<SUBKEY_ID>!` syntax.
- **cargo publish: "crate already uploaded"** — idempotent; the workflow
  detects this and skips. A real version bump fixes it.
- **Homebrew cask fails to download** — the `sha256` mismatched. Either
  the release artifact naming changed (check `tauri.conf.json` product
  name) or the artifact wasn't uploaded. Re-run `publish-cask.yml`.
- **Nightly skipped with "no commits in last 24h"** — by design. Use
  `workflow_dispatch` to force a build.

content.text:
I have enough context. The current `nightly.yml` already has a "no commits in 24h" skip gate, a separate `latest.yml` for rolling-from-main, and `publish-cask.yml` / `publish-crates.yml` that are only called from stable `release.yml`. Now let me ask clarifying questions.

**Question 1 of ~4:** The skip-gate in the current `nightly.yml` compares to "commits in the last 24h." You now want to skip if there are no changes since the *last nightly build*, which is stricter and more accurate (manual re-runs of a stale main would otherwise produce identical bits, wasting crates.io + tap slots). Which comparison target do you prefer?

- **A)** Last `nightly-*` GitHub release's target SHA — authoritative, survives across runners, but requires a `gh api` call.
- **B)** The git tag `nightly-YYYY-MM-DD` pointing at last nightly's commit — simple `git rev-parse`, but relies on the tag not being force-moved.
- **C)** Store a marker artifact/commit-sha on the `latest-nightly` release description — robust but adds state-management complexity.

My recommendation: **A** — `gh release list --json tagName,targetCommitish` sorted newest-first, filter to `nightly-*`, compare `targetCommitish` to `github.sha`. It's one API call, no extra state, works for manual dispatch as well.

<ide_opened_file>The user opened the file /Users/ddaniels/Dropbox/orpheus/.worktrees/dependabot-actions/docs/RELEASING.md in the IDE. This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_opened_file>

**Question 2 of ~4:** Homebrew cask naming. Casks don't actually support `--HEAD` like formulae do, so "HEAD-tracking" is done via a separate cask with `version :latest` + `sha256 :no_check` pointing at a moving URL. Three ways to expose this:

- **A)** `orpheus@nightly` — Homebrew's "versioned alias" convention. Install: `brew install --cask dougnukem/orpheus/orpheus@nightly`.
- **B)** `orpheus-nightly` — plain separate cask. Install: `brew install --cask dougnukem/orpheus/orpheus-nightly`. Slightly more common in the wild for nightly channels.
- **C)** Reuse the `orpheus` cask with a `--HEAD`-flavored URL (would break stable users). Not recommended.

My recommendation: **B** (`orpheus-nightly`). The `@` syntax in Homebrew is mostly used for pinned major versions (e.g. `python@3.11`), and a separate cask keeps stable/nightly install commands cleanly distinct.

**Question 3 of ~4:** Cargo version derivation. Workspace version is currently `0.1.0`. For the nightly crate version, two reasonable rules:

- **A)** Always patch-bump the current `Cargo.toml` workspace version → `0.1.1-nightly.20260417.abc1234` (your spec example, but patched for the actual current 0.1.0). After cutting `v0.1.1` stable, nightlies become `0.1.2-nightly.*` automatically — as long as we bump `Cargo.toml` to `0.1.1` during the release bump (which `cargo-release` already does).
- **B)** Explicit `NIGHTLY_NEXT_VERSION` stored in a file (e.g. `.nightly-version` or a workflow env) that a maintainer bumps manually when targeting a minor/major.

My recommendation: **A**. It's zero-maintenance, your spec explicitly allows "skip over 0.1.1 if we end up cutting 0.2.0 next," and `cargo-release` keeps `Cargo.toml` current so the derivation is always correct. Format: `{major}.{minor}.{patch+1}-nightly.{YYYYMMDD}.{shortSHA}` — date for human sort, SHA for exact provenance.

---SHOW #14---
commit d36bb4fa56d3cd24c06c1e75dd474728f15823ed
Author: Doug Daniels <daniels.douglas@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 17 10:53:32 2026 -0500

    feat(ci): publish rolling orpheus-head Homebrew cask from main (#14)
    
    * feat(ci): publish rolling orpheus-head Homebrew cask from main
    
    Adds a second cask (`orpheus-head`) to the `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus`
    tap that tracks the rolling `latest` pre-release on GitHub. Users can:
    
        brew install --cask dougnukem/orpheus/orpheus-head
        brew reinstall --cask orpheus-head   # pull the newest HEAD build
    
    Implementation:
    - `packaging/homebrew/orpheus-head.rb.template` uses `version :latest`
      + `sha256 :no_check` (hash changes on every push; no version bump).
      `conflicts_with` prevents co-installing the stable `orpheus` cask.
    - `.github/workflows/publish-cask-head.yml` is a `workflow_call`
      reusable workflow that locates the universal DMG on the `latest`
      release, renders the template into the tap repo, and commits only
      when the cask content actually changed.
    - `latest.yml` invokes it via `secrets: inherit` after `publish-latest`
      succeeds so every push to main refreshes the head cask.
    - `packaging/homebrew/README.md` documents the new template, tokens,
      and publish flow.
    
    All `${{ github.* }}` context values are routed through `env:` blocks
    and used only as shell variables, matching the injection-safe pattern
    established by the rest of the CI.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: 126ed254fb92
    
    * fix(web): remove dead ExtractPanel placeholder
    
    The redesign in d45f1c3 removed the "extract" tab from TabId but left
    behind a `{false && <ExtractPanel ... />}` guard and the ExtractPanel
    component itself. ESLint's no-constant-binary-expression flagged the
    dead-branch placeholder, which has been blocking CI on main since that
    merge. Drop both the guard and the now-unused component.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: 77342db9d568

Pulled main — PR #14 already landed the `orpheus-head` cask published from `latest.yml` (rolling, tracks every push). That satisfies the "homebrew cask --HEAD" piece. Revised understanding:

**Already done on main (d36bb4f):**
- `orpheus-head` cask with `version :latest`, `sha256 :no_check`, `conflicts_with "cask: orpheus"`
- `publish-cask-head.yml` reusable workflow
- `latest.yml` now calls it after publish-latest

**Still to do (this plan):**
1. Cargo nightly publishing (`0.X.{patch+1}-nightly.YYYYMMDD.shortSHA`) wired from `nightly.yml`
2. Tighten nightly skip-gate to compare against last `nightly-*` release's `targetCommitish`
3. Update nightly release-body with install instructions (cargo + `orpheus-head` cask + direct download)
4. README restructure: install first, then CLI + example, then web, then architecture/contributing
5. `docs/RELEASING.md` updates to match

**One thing I can't verify:** you mentioned "cargo work is already getting done in another branch" — I don't see any open PR or remote branch for cargo-nightly publishing. Before I write the spec, can you confirm one of:

- **A)** No parallel cargo branch exists yet — design + implement the full cargo-nightly publish here.
- **B)** There's a branch/PR I can't see — tell me the branch name so I can rebase on it and only own the README + release-body + docs pieces.
- **C)** Someone else is actively working on it — I write the spec for coordination only (no implementation), and scope this PR to README + release-notes text + skip-gate tightening.

Which?

content.text:
## Design

### Architecture (1/5)

Two new pieces + three edits, following existing patterns:

**New files:**
- `.github/workflows/publish-crates-nightly.yml` — reusable workflow (`workflow_call`) that patches Cargo.toml to the nightly version, runs `cargo publish -p orpheus-core / -cli / -server`, never commits the patch. Mirrors `publish-crates.yml` but takes a `nightly_version` input instead of verifying against a tag.
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-nightly-cargo-publish-and-readme-design.md` — this spec.

**Edits:**
- `.github/workflows/nightly.yml` — replace 24h gate with "compare to last `nightly-*` release's `targetCommitish`"; add a `compute-version` step emitting `{major}.{minor}.{patch+1}-nightly.{YYYYMMDD}.{shortSHA}` from Cargo.toml + `github.sha`; call `publish-crates-nightly.yml` after `publish`; rewrite the release body with install instructions.
- `README.md` — restructure: (1) Install, (2) CLI + demo output, (3) Web app / desktop, (4) Supported formats & providers, (5) Architecture, (6) Security, (7) License. Contributing stays in CONTRIBUTING.md.
- `docs/RELEASING.md` — add a "Nightly crates.io channel" section: how the version is derived, how to opt-in (`cargo install orpheus-cli --version "0.X.Y-nightly" --locked`), idempotency, and skip-gate semantics.

**Boundaries:**
- Version derivation lives in `nightly.yml` (inline script) — single consumer, small logic, no shared helper.
- `publish-crates-nightly.yml` is parallel to `publish-crates.yml`, not a refactor of it. They share nothing — stable publishing keeps its "tag must match workspace version" invariant, and nightly gets its own "tag doesn't matter, version is computed" flow. Refactoring both into one workflow would entangle two very different invariants; we keep them isolated.
- No change to `latest.yml`, no change to the `orpheus-head` cask (already does the "HEAD install" job), no new `orpheus-nightly` cask.

Does this structure look right before I detail the version-derivation and skip-gate logic?
